summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple drone attacks across Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Donetsk and other regions, targeting infrastructure and causing casualties. Russian authorities noted a large number of Ukrainian drones intercepted and reported fires from drone strikes in Russian regions. Both sides continue intensive aerial operations with guided bombs and UAVs.
key_points: Ukrainian units in Kostiantinovka face intensified pressure with up to 300 troops blocked south of the city, hindering withdrawal attempts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30887).
Russian forces claim to have destroyed six Russian UAV launch points (Source: @UkraineNow/81905).
Three 250‑kg FAB‑250 bombs were dropped on Sloviansk, injuring at least five people including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/81898).
Ukrainian drones struck a railway station in Lozova, damaging locomotives and wounding the driver and assistant (Source: @UkraineNow/81912).
Russian authorities reported a fire in a private house in Smolensk region caused by a UAV, injuring an elderly woman (Source: @tass_agency/380568).
Ukrainian guided bomb launches were reported over Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/65158).
A large queue of people awaiting crossing into Poland formed at a checkpoint in Lviv region (Source: @tass_agency/380597).
Russian officials said 249 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Russian territory (Source: @tass_agency/380570).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces are conducting extensive UAV and guided‑bomb attacks across multiple fronts, striking railway infrastructure, industrial sites and residential areas, while Russian defenses report high UAV interception rates. Significant civilian casualties were reported from Russian bombings in Sloviansk and a UAV‑induced fire in Smolensk. The situation remains fluid with both sides intensifying aerial operations.

points: Ukrainian troops in Kostiantinovka are under heavy pressure, with a blocked group of up to 300 personnel south of the city and attempts to evacuate disguised as civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30887).
Ukrainian forces launched guided aerial bombs over Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions, indicating use of precision strike capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/65158).
Ukrainian drones attacked the Lozova railway station, damaging locomotives and injuring the driver and assistant, showing targeting of transport logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81912).
Multiple UAV sightings were reported in Kharkiv, Sumy, and Donetsk directions, including a group moving from Lozivske towards Pavlograd (Source: @kpszsu/65132).
Ukrainian attacks on the Russian chemical plant AZOT in Novomoskovsk were claimed, suggesting strikes on high‑value industrial targets (Source: @eRadarrua/96491).
Russian authorities said 249 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Russian territory, reflecting intensive air defence activity (Source: @tass_agency/380570).
A UAV caused a fire in a private house in Smolensk region, injuring an elderly woman (Source: @tass_agency/380568).
Three 250‑kg FAB‑250 bombs were dropped on Sloviansk, causing injuries and damage to over 20 apartment blocks (Source: @UkraineNow/81898).
A fire broke out at a fuel‑storage facility in Yaroslavl region after UAV debris struck the site (Source: @tass_agency/380565).
Russian officials reported a large queue of people trying to leave Ukraine at a checkpoint in Lviv region, indicating humanitarian pressure (Source: @tass_agency/380597).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over Kharkiv region heading north‑west from Bogoduhivka and south‑west from the same district, showing multi‑directional pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65124, @kpszsu/65148).
Guided bomb launches were reported over eastern Donetsk areas, indicating strikes deeper into Russian‑held territory (Source: @kpszsu/65128).
Ukrainian drones attacked the railway station in Lozova, a key logistics hub in the east (Source: @UkraineNow/81912).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted heading toward Pavlograd in Dnipropetrovsk from Lozivske, suggesting coordination across the eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/65132).
Russian reports of 249 UAVs shot down include engagements over eastern Russian regions bordering Ukraine (Source: @tass_agency/380570).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over Sumy region heading from the north‑west toward Lebedyn and from the north toward Sumy city (Source: @kpszsu/65122, @kpszsu/65139).
A UAV strike in Smolensk region (northwest of Ukraine) caused a house fire and civilian injury (Source: @tass_agency/380568).
Ukrainian drone sightings in Chernihiv region near Beremitske indicate activity near the northern border (Source: @eRadarrua/96483).
Russian reports of UAV debris hitting fuel storage in Yaroslavl suggest defensive actions in the north‑central Russian zone (Source: @tass_agency/380565).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over Kharkiv heading north‑west from Bogoduhivka, close to the northern front line (Source: @kpszsu/65124).
Ukrainian drones attacked the railway station in Lozova (southern Kharkiv region) and caused damage to locomotives (Source: @UkraineNow/81912).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over Zaporizhzhia from the south‑west, indicating pressure on the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/65126).
Three FAB‑250 bombs were dropped on Sloviansk, a city in the Donetsk region, causing civilian injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81898).
Ukrainian attacks on the Russian chemical plant AZOT in Novomoskovsk (southern Russian territory) were claimed (Source: @eRadarrua/96491).
Ukrainian drone activity was noted over the Black Sea near the Crimean coast, suggesting southern maritime operations (Source: @eRadarrua/96498).
Russian defence ministries reported shooting down 249 Ukrainian UAVs across Russian regions (Source: @tass_agency/380570).
Ukrainian forces employed guided aerial bombs over Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk, showing expanded air‑to‑ground strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/65158).
Multiple UAV strikes caused fires in Russian civilian infrastructure, including a house in Smolensk and fuel storage in Yaroslavl (Source: @tass_agency/380568, @tass_agency/380565).
Ukrainian drone attacks targeted railway logistics at Lozova, disrupting transport and causing personnel casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81912).
Russian reports of UAV debris impacting industrial sites indicate ongoing contest for air superiority over strategic assets (Source: @tass_agency/380565).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces face intensified pressure in Kostiantinovka with a blocked contingent of up to 300 troops south of the city, limiting withdrawal options (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30887).
Russian defence claims 249 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down, reflecting high‑intensity aerial engagements (Source: @tass_agency/380570).
Three FAB‑250 bombs struck Sloviansk, injuring civilians and damaging over 20 residential buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81898).
Ukrainian guided bomb launches over Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk indicate expanded precision strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/65158).
Ukrainian drone attack on Lozova railway station damaged locomotives and wounded railway personnel (Source: @UkraineNow/81912).
UAV‑induced fire in Smolensk region injured an elderly woman, showing cross‑border strike reach (Source: @tass_agency/380568).
Ukrainian drones reportedly struck the Russian AZOT chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, targeting high‑value industrial infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/96491).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces are intensifying UAV and guided‑bomb operations across multiple fronts, targeting logistics, industrial sites and civilian infrastructure, which raises the risk of broader civilian casualties and infrastructure disruption. Russian air defences are responding with high interception rates, but UAV incursions continue to reach deep into Russian territory, indicating a persistent threat to rear‑area assets. The use of larger munitions such as FAB‑250 bombs by Russia in populated areas escalates the humanitarian impact and may provoke further retaliatory strikes.
sections.regional_response: Western officials continue to publicly support Ukraine, with diplomatic talks hinted at G7 meetings, while Russian authorities emphasize defensive successes and civilian impacts of Ukrainian attacks. Local Russian governors report civilian injuries and property damage, prompting emergency responses and heightened security measures around critical infrastructure. Ukrainian leadership acknowledges the strain on front‑line units, especially in contested zones like Kostiantinovka, suggesting possible future reallocations of forces.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows a pattern of multi‑directional attacks from north‑west, south‑west and southern approaches, suggesting diversified launch sites. The reported blockage of Ukrainian troops in Kostiantinovka may indicate a potential encirclement that could affect operational flexibility in the Kharkiv sector. Russian claims of high UAV loss rates should be cross‑checked with independent imagery to assess actual air‑defence effectiveness. Continued civilian casualties from both sides underscore the need for updated humanitarian risk assessments.
